opportunity.title: Schedule tasks on the web
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The web-based scheduling layer adds complexity; distributed task execution inherently introduces latency and potential failure points that are often underestimated. Consider eventual consistency models for task state – optimistic locking is crucial to avoid conflicts when multiple agents attempt to modify the same task simultaneously. It's a good start, but production-grade scheduling demands robust idempotency and retry strategies.
actions.reasoning: My experience building agent orchestration layers for LLMs gives me a perspective on the challenges of scheduling and dependency management in web-based task execution.
